reviews.review_description: hi, I'm glad to see a chart for beginners. I'm not going to nitpick but found a glaring mistake. all the galaxies are labeled to have a distance of 630 LY. at least the 4 I tried so far. a lot of useful information etc except that. it should be updated with proper info. also hope to see more nebulae and such.  keep up the good work.

I cant get off this one. Stunning graphics, smooth, and immersive.
 Love this so much, my mother just bought an oculus. She was part if the Apollo program and really loving this.
I can spend way to long flitting around space, worthwhile getting this app
Never successfully managed to install despite many attempts

developer_response.body: We are looking into this and will try to resolve this ASAP. Apologies for this issue and thanks for your patience. The Star Chart Team.

reviews.review_description: This is a great app for learning the night sky. There are a few other things to do as well but this is worth it just for the star chart. It would be nice to see more options in the chart like being able to make a more realistic view based on current location light pollution.
For anyone interested in our solar system and beyond this app has the lot excellent graphics and very informative brilliant piece of work
Downloaded but will not install

reviews.review_title: Love this app- minus one little detail
reviews.review_description:
Great app all around! Love the graphics, and the fact that the chart is in real time, by location. I could watch my little private planetarium for hours!

One very small suggestion: it would be great if the moon could show its current phase, with data regarding. Other than that, it's perfect! Much appreciated for developing this.
Worth the price..
Love the little I've explored.

reviews.review_title: Lack of time change lets it down.
reviews.review_description: Love most of the app. Solar system orrery is fantastic travelling around the planets. Planetarium view is really effective, but it only shows the current sky view. There doesn't seem to be a way of setting the view to see what the sky will look like at any other time or day, unless I'm missing a control that's hidden somewhere!

Cant install on S10.
Please update.

reviews.review_title: Great app, but has some inaccuracies
reviews.review_description:
First, the bad: This app is a lot of fun, but I'm having trouble trusting everything it's telling me. Most of the really far away deep sky objects have very inaccurate distances displayed. The Andromeda galaxy, for instance, is labeled as being 630 LY away. It is, in fact, 2.5 million light years away. The Sombrero galaxy? The app says 630 LY again,  but it's closer to 29 million. 630 LY ISS still very much within the Milky Way. That data is aggressively wrong. 
The good? Everything else. It's fun exploring the solar system, traveling to stars like Betelgeuse, visiting the dwarf planets, etc. I'm an astronomy nerd, so despite those glaring errors, it was still immensely exciting.
Whenever I try to download it it it will download and then start installing, then say failed to install. Waste of $5. Galaxy S8+ btw.

Wow! This is what VR should be more about because the concept of space is truly amazing. In this app, you can travel to many areas that are not limited to our planets. You can travel to other stars and preview them before heading there. Also, plenty of scientific stats of each star or planet you view. Highly recommend!
Tried installing several times and it fails to do so. This is when a return policy should be in order. Selling a digital product that does not work should be illegal is it not?
I get a "will not install" message. Please advise
I put this app on my tablet and loved it.  Using it interactively is Iso much more.  It is the best space experience I've had thus far.

Cannot install on Samsung Galaxy S8. Paid for the app. Saw that this has been an issue since March, it is now nearing the end of June and still nothing has been resolved it seems... don't buy until it gets fixed.

reviews.review_title: The most fun I've had with VR yet!
reviews.review_description:
I can't vouch for the scientific accuracy of all of the data, but I can't deny that I have had a boatload of fun just flying around the solar system. It gives a good sense space and position of heavenly bodies, although I do somewhat question the scale that everything is presented in.

Apps like this are exactly why I purchased my Oculus Go, and I can't wait to pick it up again.
